title: rosa canina var. oxyphylla
intro: Rosa canina var. oxyphylla, also known as the dwarf dog rose or European dwarf rose, is a variety of the Rosa canina species, a small shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Rosa canina var. oxyphylla include supporting eye health, reducing inflammation, and providing relief from digestive issues. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as rose hips that are rich in vitamins and minerals.
